topic: If Clauses / Conditionals Part 2 — Second & Third Conditional
keyPointsSummary: Second conditional talks about imaginary/hypothetical present or future situations
Third conditional talks about imaginary past situations that did not actually happen — often regret
Use 'were' instead of 'was' in formal second conditional (If I were you...)

quickQuiz.hindi: अगर मेरे पास पैसे होते, तो मैं यह घर खरीदता।
quickQuiz.answer: If I had money, I would buy this house.

quickQuiz.hindi: अगर मैं तुम होता, तो मैं यह नहीं करता।
quickQuiz.answer: If I were you, I would not do this.

quickQuiz.hindi: अगर उसे पता होता, तो वह आती।
quickQuiz.answer: If she knew, she would come.

quickQuiz.hindi: अगर मुझे समय मिलता, तो मैं travel करता।
quickQuiz.answer: If I had time, I would travel.

quickQuiz.hindi: अगर मैंने मेहनत की होती, तो मैं पास हो जाता।
quickQuiz.answer: If I had worked hard, I would have passed.

quickQuiz.hindi: अगर तुमने मुझे बताया होता, तो मैं मदद करता।
quickQuiz.answer: If you had told me, I would have helped.

quickQuiz.hindi: अगर वह जल्दी आया होता, तो हम मिल पाते।
quickQuiz.answer: If he had come early, we would have met.

quickQuiz.hindi: अगर मुझे गलती पता होती, तो मैं इसे सुधार देता।
quickQuiz.answer: If I had known the mistake, I would have corrected it.

quickQuiz.hindi: अगर मौसम अच्छा होता, तो हम बाहर जाते।
quickQuiz.answer: If the weather were good, we would go out.

quickQuiz.hindi: अगर उसने मुझसे पूछा होता, तो मैं बता देता।
quickQuiz.answer: If he had asked me, I would have told him.

mustRememberRules: Second conditional talks about imaginary/hypothetical present or future situations
Third conditional talks about imaginary past situations that did not actually happen — often regret
Use 'were' instead of 'was' in formal second conditional (If I were you...)
